info.description: When supplying materials to production, you can use staging requests to make required materials available. You can stage the full quantity of a material at once or stage partial quantities of the material at intervals over a period of time. This API service allows you to perform activities such as staging materials or retrieving staging requests in integration with Extended Warehouse Management.
info.title: Extended Warehouse Management Staging

tags.name: Material Staging
tags.description: Allows you to perform material staging related activities.

post.summary: Retrieves all staging requests for a given plant.
post.description: Retrieves all staging requests to Extended Warehouse Management for a given plant. You can specify various parameters to refine the results.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The page number you want to query. Number 0 is the first page. Example: https://xxx?page=0&size=1.

parameters.name: size
parameters.description: The page size determines how many records are displayed in one page. An internal validation limits the maximum value to 100. If not specified, the default value is 20.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: The retrieved records can be sorted using one or more of the following fields: 'createdDateTime', 'stagingRequestNumber', 'stagingRequestItemNumber', 'status', 'material', 'warehouseNumber', 'productionSupplyArea', 'stagingMethod', 'order', 'operation', 'modifiedDateTime' and 'plannedDeliveryDateTime'. By default, the staging requests are sorted by 'modifiedDateTime' in ascending order and 'stagingRequestItemNumber' in ascending order. Example: 'https://xxx?xxxx&sort=modifiedDateTime,desc&sort=operation,desc'.

400.description: The HTTP request is invalid and cannot be processed by the server.

401.description: You are not authorized to call this resource.

403.description: Accessing the resource you are trying to reach is forbidden.

404.description: One of the related elements is not found.

post.description: Discards multiple staging requests for a given plant. You can only discard staging requests that have Delivered status.

400.description: The HTTP request is invalid and cannot be processed by the server.

401.description: You are not authorized to call this resource.

403.description: Accessing the resource you are trying to reach is forbidden.

plant.description: The plant where the staging requests are performed.

stagingRequestNumber.description: The staging request number which uniquely identifies a staging request. You can check it in the Manage Staging app.

productionSupplyAreas.description: The list of production supply areas where staged materials are delivered to.

stagingRequestItemStatuses.description: The list of staging request item statuses. You can choose to retrieve staging requests with one of the 10 statuses: 1) 'NEW': Retrieve staging requests that are newly created; 2) 'IN_PROCESS': Retrieve staging requests that are still being processed; 3) 'DELIVERED': Retrieve staging requests that are delivered to Extended Warehouse Management; 4) 'DELIVERY_FAILED': Retrieve staging requests that failed to be delivered to Extended Warehouse Management due to technical reasons; 5) 'INITIALIZED': Retrieve staging requests that are initialized in Extended Warehouse Management; 6) 'NOT_STAGED': Retrieve staging requests where no component is staged; 7) 'PARTIALLY_STAGED': Retrieve staging requests where only part of the requested components are staged to production supply area; 8) 'STAGED': Retrieve staging requests where all requested components are successfully staged to production supply area; 9) 'OVER_STAGED': Retrieve staging requests where components staged exceed the requested quantity; 10) 'DISCARDED': Retrieve staging requests that will not be processed any further..

orders.description: The list of orders for which the staging requests are performed.

components.description: The list of components that are requested in the staging requests.

operations.description: The list of operations for which the staging requests are performed.

maxCreationTime.description: The upper limit of the creation date time range of the staging requests. Format (ISO-8601): yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSS'Z'.

minCreationTime.description: The lower limit of the creation date time range of the staging requests. Format (ISO-8601): yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSS'Z'.

maxPlannedDeliveryTime.description: The upper limit of the planned delivery date time range of the staging requests. Format (ISO-8601): yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSS'Z'.

minPlannedDeliveryTime.description: The lower limit of the planned delivery date time range of the staging requests. Format (ISO-8601): yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSS'Z'.

numberOfElements.description: The number of records in the current page

totalElements.description: The total number of records that are retrieved

totalPages.description: The total number of pages returned

stagingRequestItemNumber.description: The staging request item number in SAP Digital Manufacturing. There can be multiple staging request item numbers under a staging request number.

material.description: The material that's requested in the staging request.

materialDescription.description: The description for the material that's requested in the staging request.

materialVersion.description: The version for the material that's requested in the staging request.

productionSupplyArea.description: The production supply area where the staged material is delivered to.

receivedQuantity.description: The quantity which has already been fulfilled in the staging request. It's calculated using the unit of measure in the staging request.

stagingQuantity.description: The quantity that is requested in the staging request.

stagingUom.description: The unit of measure used in the staging request. It could be either base unit of measure, or alternate unit of measure of the material.

order.description: The order number for which the staging request is performed. It's only applicable to staging request with single-order staging method.

operation.description: The operation for which the staging request is performed.

stagingMethod.description: The staging method of the staging request. There are 2 possible staging method: 'MES_DRIVEN_CROSS_ORDER_STAGING' and 'MES_DRIVEN_SINGLE_ORDER_STAGING'.

status.description: The status of the staging request. There are 10 possible statuses: 1) 'NEW': Retrieve staging requests that are newly created; 2) 'IN_PROCESS': Retrieve staging requests that are still being processed; 3) 'DELIVERED': Retrieve staging requests that are delivered to Extended Warehouse Management; 4) 'DELIVERY_FAILED': Retrieve staging requests that failed to be delivered to Extended Warehouse Management due to technical reasons; 5) 'INITIALIZED': Retrieve staging requests that are initialized in Extended Warehouse Management; 6) 'NOT_STAGED': Retrieve staging requests where no component is staged; 7) 'PARTIALLY_STAGED': Retrieve staging requests where only part of the requested components are staged to production supply area; 8) 'STAGED': Retrieve staging requests where all requested components are successfully staged to production supply area; 9) 'OVER_STAGED': Retrieve staging requests where components staged exceed the requested quantity; 10) 'DISCARDED': Retrieve staging requests that will not be processed any further.

warehouseNumber.description: The warehouse number from which the staged materials are delivered.

batchNumber.description: The batch number of the staged material.

plannedDeliveryDateTime.description: The planned delivery date time of the staging request. Format (ISO-8601): yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSS'Z'.

createdDateTime.description: The creation date time of the staging request. Format (ISO-8601): yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSS'Z'.

modifiedDateTime.description: The modification date time of the staging request. Format (ISO-8601): yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSS'Z'.

reservationNumber.description: The reservation number of the component within the corresponding bill of materials (BOM). It's only applicable to staging requests with single-order staging method.

reservationItemNumber.description: The reservation item number of the component within the corresponding bill of materials (BOM). It's only applicable to staging requests with single-order staging method.

plant.description: The plant of the staging requests.

stagingRequestNumbers.description: The list of staging request numbers that you want to discard.

success.description: The list of staging requests that have been successfully discarded.

failure.description: The list of staging requests that failed to be discarded.

page.description: Indicates the page where staging requests are retrieved.

size.description: Specifies the number of records on one page.

sort.description: Defines how the records are sorted.

empty.description: If the value is 'true', no records were retrieved. If the value is 'false', one or more records were retrieved.

sorted.description: If the value is 'true', the retrieved records are sorted as specified. If the value is 'false', no records were retrieved.

unsorted.description: If the value is 'false', the retrieved records are sorted as specified. If the value is 'true', no records were retrieved.

code.description: Contains a technical code for the error.

correlationId.description: Contains a correlation ID for the error.

code.description: Contains a technical code for the error.

x-sap-shortText: Perform material staging related activities in Extended Warehouse Management integration.

regionHost.description: The data center where SAP Digital Manufacturing is provisioned for your organization

tokenHost.description: The authentication token server where SAP Digital Manufacturing is provisioned for your organization
